meta.title: What is an e-Invoice? The Future of Electronic Billing
meta.description: Learn what an electronic invoice (e-invoice) is, how it differs from a PDF, and why governments worldwide are making them mandatory.

blocks.title: What is an e-Invoice?
blocks.subtitle: Governments worldwide are mandating a shift from PDFs to structured electronic invoices. Learn what an e-invoice actually is and how to prepare.

blocks.authorBio: Jane is a certified CPA with over 15 years of experience in small business accounting.

blocks.heading: A PDF is NOT an e-Invoice
blocks.text: There is a massive misconception in the business world: emailing a PDF to a client is not an 'e-invoice'. A PDF is just a digital picture of a piece of paper. A true e-invoice (Electronic Invoice) is a structured data file (usually XML or EDI format) that is transmitted directly from the seller's accounting software into the buyer's accounting software, often passing through a government tax portal for automatic auditing.

summaryItems: A true e-invoice contains structured data (XML) meant to be read by computers, not humans.
Over 60 countries (including Italy, Poland, and India) have already made e-invoicing mandatory for B2B transactions to prevent tax fraud.
E-invoicing eliminates manual data entry, reducing accounting errors by over 90%.

blocks.title: PDF Invoice vs. e-Invoice
rows: Real-time automatic reporting to the government
Being phased out in the EU

questions.question: Do small businesses in the US need to use e-invoices?
questions.description: Currently, no. The US has not mandated e-invoicing for B2B transactions (unlike the EU). A standard PDF invoice is perfectly legal and standard for small businesses in the US, UK, and Canada.

blocks.heading: Not ready for XML?
blocks.subtext: If you don't live in a mandated country, a standard PDF is still the best option. Use our free generator to create one instantly.
